story.iWant: integrate Keycloak OIDC authentication with JWT validation and role-based access control
story.soThat: the application can authenticate users via OAuth2/OIDC and enforce RBAC for API endpoints based on Keycloak roles
story.tasks: - Task 1: Add OAuth2 Resource Server dependency - Task 2: Configure Keycloak connection in application.yml - Task 3: Create SecurityConfig.java - Task 4: Create JwtValidator.java - Task 5: Create RoleMapper.java - Task 6: Configure Keycloak realm and roles - Task 7: Create integration test for authentication flow - Task 8: Update documentation and verify build

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Spring Security OAuth2 Resource Server dependency (spring-boot-starter-oauth2-resource-server) is added to build.gradle.kts
SecurityConfig.java is created in the security module configuring JWT validation with Keycloak issuer URI and JWK set endpoint
JwtValidator.java service is implemented to decode JWT tokens and extract claims (subject, roles, email)
RoleMapper.java service is implemented to map Keycloak realm roles to application roles: ADMIN, ITSM_AGENT, DEVELOPER, MANAGER, EMPLOYEE
/actuator/health endpoint is secured and returns 200 OK only with valid JWT Bearer token
RBAC roles are configured in Keycloak realm: ADMIN, ITSM_AGENT, DEVELOPER, MANAGER, EMPLOYEE
Integration test validates complete authentication flow: obtain JWT from Keycloak → call secured endpoint → verify role mapping → assert 200 OK response
Integration test validates unauthorized access returns 401 Unauthorized without JWT token

doc.title: Epic 1 Foundation Tech Spec - Story 1.4
doc.section: Week 1: Project Setup & Database Foundation - Story 1.4
doc.snippet:
Story 1.4: Integrate Keycloak OIDC authentication - Add spring-boot-starter-oauth2-resource-server dependency - Configure SecurityConfig.java with JWT validation - Create JwtValidator.java to decode and validate Keycloak tokens - Create RoleMapper.java to map Keycloak roles to application roles - Add integration test: authenticate with Keycloak, validate JWT, check roles - Configure RBAC: Admin, ITSM_Agent, Developer, Manager, Employee
Acceptance Criteria: - ✅ Authenticated /actuator/health endpoint returns 200 OK with JWT token - ✅ Integration test validates Keycloak JWT and RBAC roles

doc.title: Security Module Package Structure
doc.snippet: security/ # Security Module (authentication & authorization) ├── api/ # Public security APIs │ ├── JwtValidator.java │ ├── RoleMapper.java │ └── BatchAuthService.java ├── internal/ │ ├── KeycloakClient.java │ └── AclCacheManager.java ├── config/ # Spring Security configuration │ └── SecurityConfig.java └── package-info.java

doc.title: Module Boundaries - Security Module
doc.snippet:
Security Module: - May Depend On: audit - Provides @NamedInterface: io.monosense.synergyflow.security.api.BatchAuthService - Used by: itsm, pm, workflow, sse modules
Enforcement: - Cross-module calls must go via spi/ packages annotated with @NamedInterface - internal/ packages are not referenced from other modules

doc.title: Technology Stack - Spring Security
doc.snippet: - Spring Security 6.x + OAuth2 Resource Server - Keycloak integration for authentication and authorization - JWT token validation with JWK endpoint - Stateless authentication (no sessions)

doc.title: Testing Strategy - Integration Tests
doc.snippet:
Integration Tests (Spring Boot Test + Testcontainers)
Scope: Test database interactions, outbox pattern, Event Worker, SSE gateway, batch auth
Testcontainers Configuration: @SpringBootTest @Testcontainers
Examples: - OutboxIntegrationTest.java - EventWorkerIntegrationTest.java - SseGatewayIntegrationTest.java - ReadModelIntegrationTest.java

doc.title: Testing Strategy - Unit Tests
doc.snippet:
Unit Tests (JUnit 5 + Mockito)
Target Coverage: >80% for domain logic
Examples: - TicketServiceTest.java - SlaCalculatorTest.java - RuleEvaluatorTest.java - BatchAuthServiceTest.java: Test batch authorization with mocked Keycloak
Run: ./gradlew test

doc.title: Application Configuration
doc.snippet: spring: application: name: synergyflow datasource: url: jdbc:postgresql://${DB_HOST:localhost}:${DB_PORT:5432}/${DB_NAME:synergyflow} jpa: database-platform: org.hibernate.dialect.PostgreSQLDialect flyway: enabled: true locations: classpath:db/migration

doc.title: Product Requirements - Security & RBAC
doc.snippet: RBAC roles required for the platform: - Admin: Full system access - ITSM_Agent: Ticket/incident management operations - Developer: PM/issue tracking operations - Manager: Approval workflow operations - Employee: Self-service request submission

file.reason: Security module is already declared with @ApplicationModule annotation. This story adds authentication functionality to this existing module structure.
Current dependencies include Spring Boot starters (web, data-jpa), Spring Modulith, PostgreSQL, Flyway. Need to add spring-boot-starter-oauth2-resource-server for OAuth2/OIDC support.
Reference integration test pattern using Testcontainers. Story 1.4 integration test should follow similar pattern with Keycloak container or WireMock.
Need to add OAuth2 Resource Server configuration properties: spring.security.oauth2.resourceserver.jwt.issuer-uri and jwk-set-uri

constraints.constraint: Security module must follow modulith pattern with api/ package for public interfaces and internal/ package for implementation details (enforced by ArchUnit tests)
Security module is a leaf module - depends only on audit module, provides services to itsm, pm, workflow, sse modules via @NamedInterface APIs
Application acts as OAuth2 Resource Server (not Authorization Server) - Keycloak handles token issuance
Stateless authentication via JWT Bearer tokens - no server-side session management
JWT validation uses JWK public key from Keycloak JWKS endpoint for signature verification
Integration tests must use Testcontainers for realistic environment simulation or WireMock as lightweight alternative
Unit tests must achieve >80% coverage for domain logic (JwtValidator, RoleMapper services)
All external service URLs must use environment variable placeholders with sensible defaults for local development

interface.name: JwtValidator
interface.description: Public API for validating JWT tokens and extracting claims (subject, roles, email). Used by other modules for authentication.

interface.name: RoleMapper
interface.description: Public API for mapping Keycloak realm roles to application roles. Used for RBAC enforcement.

interface.name: SecurityFilterChain
interface.description: Spring Security configuration bean defining authentication and authorization rules for HTTP endpoints.

interface.name: JwtDecoder
interface.description: Auto-configured bean that validates JWT signatures using Keycloak JWK endpoint. Injected into JwtValidator service.

tests.standards: Unit tests use JUnit 5 with Mockito for mocking dependencies. Integration tests use Spring Boot Test with Testcontainers for realistic environment simulation. Test classes follow naming convention *Test.java for unit tests and *IntegrationTest.java for integration tests. Target code coverage is >80% for domain logic. All tests run via ./gradlew test and must pass before commits.

ideas.idea: Unit test to verify spring-boot-starter-oauth2-resource-server dependency is present in classpath (can check for JwtDecoder bean availability)
Unit test for SecurityConfig: verify SecurityFilterChain bean is created with correct configuration (OAuth2 resource server enabled, /actuator/health secured)
Unit test for JwtValidator: mock JwtDecoder, provide sample JWT claims, verify extraction of sub, roles, email fields. Test error handling for invalid/expired tokens.
Unit test for RoleMapper: provide various Keycloak role lists, verify correct mapping to application roles. Test case-insensitive matching and unknown role handling.
Integration test: start application with Testcontainers, attempt to access /actuator/health without token (expect 401), then with valid JWT (expect 200 OK)
Manual setup task + optional integration test to verify Keycloak realm configuration via Keycloak Admin API
Integration test SecurityIntegrationTest: use Keycloak Testcontainer or WireMock to mock JWKS endpoint, obtain JWT token, call secured endpoint, verify role extraction and 200 OK response
Integration test SecurityIntegrationTest: send request to /actuator/health without Authorization header, assert 401 Unauthorized response
